pattern: A Chebyshev ball of radius k is a square of side 2k+1, so rows and columns tile independently and the count is the product of the two ceilings.
hints: What shape of cells does one sensor cover under Chebyshev distance? It is a square block.
Because the coverage is a square, the rows and columns can be tiled separately.
Tile n rows and m columns each into blocks of length 2k+1 and multiply the two ceilings.

edge_inputs.description: five by five with radius one
radius zero needs one per cell

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [rng.randint(1, 1000), rng.randint(1, 1000), rng.randint(0, 1000)]
